based_on: Byte-precise mutation of fixtures/mazur.golden.jsonl (engine-emitted canonical bytes). The fixture differs from the golden in exactly two places: the fixture_status block (3 fields) and the single targeted numeric value.

what_was_NOT_mutated: parameters_before.w5 (0.4 — left operand of Rule 7 sum)
updates[4].update (-0.0410835203 — right operand of Rule 7 sum)
updates[4].weight_after (0.358916480 — Rule 6 still passes because it doesn't read parameters_after)
all other parameter_after entries (only w5 mutated)

single_target_invariant: Reconciliation produces exactly one Rule 7 failure naming parameters_after.w5. Rule 6 must NOT fire (updates[4].weight_after is internally consistent with weight_before + update). No other rule fires.
reconciliation_check_targeted_first: Rule 7: parameters_after[param] == parameters_before[param] + sum(updates targeting param)
failure_priority_assertion: Per docs/reconciliation.md, reconciliation failures report before fixture-lifecycle failures.
bias_edge_case_note: This fixture targets the weight branch of Rule 7 (parameter id appears in some updates[i].parameter_id). The bias branch (parameter not in any update, bias_policy.mode === 'constant', exact-zero-delta) is exercised by test/reconcile.bad-params-after.test.ts subtest (b) via in-memory mutation rather than a dedicated fixture, because the bias case requires a sub-tolerance delta that this fixture would not exercise.
purpose: Anti-circularity (Csmith / CompCert lineage): proves the reconciler checks Rule 7 (final state == parameters_before + sum(updates)) by detecting a 1e-6 mismatch (1000x the 1e-9 tolerance) between stored parameters_after.w5 and the sum of (unmutated) parameters_before.w5 + updates[4].update.
